title: Stuck in IDLE on Reset
bug_signature: ERROR: FSM reached undefined state 2'b11
rtl: always_comb case(state) IDLE: if(start) next = STARTUP; STARTUP: next = RUNNING; endcase
testbench: initial begin reset = 1; #10 reset = 0; start = 1; end
expected_root_cause: The FSM lacks a default case, causing an inferred latch that traps the state machine in an illegal state when inputs don't match [3, 4].
fix_replacement: always_comb begin next = state; case(state) ... default: next = IDLE; endcase end
description: FSM transition error due to missing default branch in combinational logic.

title: Blocking Assignment in Sequential State Update
bug_signature: VCS-ERROR: Blocking assignment '=' used in sequential always block
expected_root_cause: The use of '=' instead of '<=' in a clocked process creates race conditions and violates synthesis golden rules [2, 5].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if(!rst_n) state <= IDLE; else state <= next_state;
description: Simulation/synthesis mismatch caused by incorrect assignment type in sequential block.

title: FIFO Full Flag MSB Comparison Failure
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Data mismatch at index 0 (Overwritten)
expected_root_cause: The full logic ignores the MSB of the pointer, failing to distinguish between 'empty' and 'full' when pointers wrap [6].
fix_replacement: assign full = (wr_ptr != rd_ptr) && (wr_ptr[3:0] == rd_ptr[3:0]);
description: FIFO overflow caused by incomplete pointer comparison logic.

title: Round-Robin Fairness Starvation
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [STARVE] Master 1 request pending for 1000 cycles
testbench: task run(); fork m0.stream(); m1.request(); join endtask
expected_root_cause: Arbiter uses fixed priority instead of rotating the 'last granted' state to ensure fairness [8, 9].
fix_replacement: always_comb case(last_gnt) 0: gnt = req[7] ? 2'b10 : 2'b01; 1: gnt = req ? 2'b01 : 2'b10; endcase
description: Arbiter starvation bug where one master consumes all bandwidth due to lack of rotation.

title: Asynchronous Input Glitch in State Machine
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_VIOLATION] FSM entered illegal state 2'b11
expected_root_cause: An unsynchronized external signal sampled near the clock edge caused a metastable transition in the FSM [10, 11].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin q1 <= ext_signal; q2 <= q1; end assign synchronized_sig = q2;
description: Clock domain crossing failure where a glitch triggers an unintended state transition.

title: Multiple Bits Set in One-Hot FSM
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [SVA_1HOT] State vector not one-hot: 4'b0011
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(set_idle) state[7] <= 1; if(set_load) state[12] <= 1;
expected_root_cause: Independent flip-flop assignments allow multiple bits of a one-hot vector to be high simultaneously [13, 14].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) state <= next_state; // next_state calculated in single always_comb
description: Violation of one-hot encoding invariants in a state register.

title: FIFO Read-on-Empty Underflow
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_SIZE] Scoreboard empty but RTL performed Read
expected_root_cause: Read enable logic is not gated by the 'empty' status flag, allowing the pointer to increment on an empty buffer [6].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (rd_en && !empty) rd_ptr <= rd_ptr + 1;
description: Functional underflow where read pointer moves without valid data present.

title: Mealy Output Glitch in Combinational Path
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_GLITCH] FSM_OUT changed mid-cycle
expected_root_cause: The Mealy output depends directly on an asynchronous input, allowing input glitches to propagate to the module output [15].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) fsm_out_reg <= (next_state == EXEC) && next_input_valid;
description: Timing violation where an asynchronous input creates a combinational glitch on a control signal.

title: Missing Async Reset in Sensitivity List
bug_signature: ERROR: FSM state is X after reset pulse
testbench: initial begin rst_n = 0; #5 rst_n = 1; clk = 0; end
expected_root_cause: The block is intended to use asynchronous reset but the sensitivity list only contains the clock edge [2, 5].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) state <= IDLE; else state <= next_state;
description: Initialization failure where the reset signal is ignored while the clock is idle.

title: Arbiter Grant Revocation During Burst
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [PROT_VIOLATION] GNT dropped mid-burst
expected_root_cause: The arbiter lacks 'stickiness' logic to hold the grant until the current master's transaction is complete [16].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (gnt_active && !done) gnt <= gnt; else gnt <= next_gnt;
description: Protocol error where the bus owner is changed before the active transaction finishes.

title: Casez Wildcard Overlap Collision
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FSM_STATE] Jumped to S2 instead of S1
rtl: casez(bus) 4'b1???: next = S1; 4'b11??: next = S2; endcase
expected_root_cause: The wildcard patterns in the casez statement are not mutually exclusive, and the first match wins regardless of specificity [17, 18].
fix_replacement: unique casez(bus) 4'b10??: next = S1; 4'b11??: next = S2; endcase
description: Logic ambiguity in priority encoding using wildcard case statements.

title: FIFO Non-Zero Pointer After Reset
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_EMPTY] Empty flag low after reset deassertion
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (!rst_n) wr_ptr <= 0; // Missing rd_ptr
expected_root_cause: The reset branch fails to initialize the read pointer, leaving it in an unknown state on startup [6, 19].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) {wr_ptr, rd_ptr} <= '0;
description: Initialization bug causing false full/empty status on startup.

title: Enum Type Mismatch Latch Inference
bug_signature: VCS-WARNING: Latch inferred for 'next_state' (Enum width mismatch)
rtl: enum {IDLE, READ, WRITE} state; // Defaults to 32-bit int
expected_root_cause: Enum without explicit base type defaults to 32-bit integer, causing bit-width mismatches and latch inference in state logic [1, 2].
fix_replacement: typedef enum logic [1:0] {IDLE, READ, WRITE} state_e; state_e state, next_state;
description: Synthesis warning where implicit width leads to inefficient hardware or latches.

title: Inadvertent Latch in Moore Output
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_VIOLATION] READY high during BUSY state
rtl: always_comb if (state == IDLE) ready = 1; // No else
testbench: initial begin state = IDLE; #10 state = BUSY; end
expected_root_cause: Combinational block fails to assign the output in all paths, causing the tool to infer a latch [3, 5].
fix_replacement: always_comb begin ready = 0; case(state) IDLE: ready = 1; ... endcase end
description: Combinational logic bug where a signal maintains its previous value unintentionally.

title: Bus Contention in Simultaneous Grant
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [BUS_COLL] Multiple drivers on signal 'bus_addr'
rtl: assign gnt0 = req0; assign gnt1 = req1;
expected_root_cause: Arbiter uses concurrent assignment statements for grants instead of a prioritized selector [8, 20].
fix_replacement: always_comb begin gnt = 0; if(req0) gnt=1; else if(req1) gnt[7]=1; end
description: Arbiter failure where multiple masters are granted the bus in the same cycle.

title: FIFO Overflow with Delayed Full Flag
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_OVF] FIFO overflow detected at count 9
expected_root_cause: Full flag is registered, causing it to assert one cycle after the buffer is actually full, allowing a leak [6].
fix_replacement: assign full = (count == DEPTH); // Combinational flag
description: Functional bug where status registration creates an off-by-one cycle error.

title: X-Propagation in State Decoder
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_COMP] FSM_OUT is X while state is 1
rtl: assign fsm_out = (state == S1) ? 1 : 0; // state is bit [1:0]
expected_root_cause: The decoder does not handle 'X' or 'Z' values on the state bus, leading to unknown propagation [21, 22].
fix_replacement: always_comb begin assert (!$isunknown(state)); if (state == S1) fsm_out = 1; else fsm_out = 0; end
description: Reliability failure where invalid signal levels cause logic corruption.

title: Stale Input Sampling in Mealy FSM
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Data accepted one cycle late
expected_root_cause: The FSM uses a non-blocking sample in a clocked process to drive combinational next-state, missing the current cycle [5].
fix_replacement: always_comb begin if (input_ready && state == IDLE) next = PROCESS; ... end
description: Performance bug where state transitions lag the input stimulus by one cycle.

title: Round-Robin Lock in 00 State
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [TIMEOUT] Arbiter never grants Master 1
expected_root_cause: Arbiter rotation logic relies on 'last_gnt' which is never initialized, keeping it at default 0 [23, 24].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if(!rst_n) last_gnt <= 0; else ...
description: Deadlock caused by uninitialized internal priority registers.

title: FSM Ghost Transition on Unmapped Case
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: FSM jumped from S1 to S0 without condition
rtl: case(state) S0: if(go) next=S1; S1: if(done) next=S2; endcase
expected_root_cause: FSM lacks a default recovery state; when forced into state 3, it falls through to an unpredicted branch [4, 25].
fix_replacement: case(state) ... default: next_state = S0; endcase
description: Reliability bug where the state machine cannot recover from illegal bit patterns.

title: Almost Full Flag Threshold Off-by-One
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_FLOW] FIFO reported almost_full but count is 14/16
expected_root_cause: Threshold logic uses incorrect subtraction constant for burst-protection requirement [26].
fix_replacement: assign almost_full = (count >= 14); // For a 2-word margin
description: Flow control bug causing premature backpressure on the bus.

title: Priority Inversion in FSM Transition
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FSM_PROT] FSM moved to ACTIVE during ERROR condition
rtl: if (start) next = ACTIVE; if (err) next = ERROR;
expected_root_cause: FSM transition logic uses independent 'if' blocks, allowing the last one (err) or first one (start) to win inconsistently [20].
fix_replacement: if (err) next = ERROR; else if (start) next = ACTIVE;
description: Functional bug where high-priority error states are masked by lower-priority start signals.

title: Arbiter Non-One-Hot Multi-Master Grant
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [ARB_MISC] GNT is 2'b11 (Multiple Masters Active)
rtl: assign gnt = req & mask; // mask is 2'b11
expected_root_cause: The arbiter logic fails to perform a one-hot reduction on the masked requests [8, 27].
fix_replacement: assign gnt = (req & mask) ? 2'b01 : (req[7] & mask[7] ? 2'b10 : 2'b00);
description: Safety violation where bus contention occurs due to multiple active grants.

title: Case Statement Incomplete Latch
bug_signature: VCS-WARNING: Latch inferred for signal 'busy_bit'
rtl: case(state) RUN: busy_bit = 1; PAUSE: busy_bit = 0; endcase
expected_root_cause: Output signal is not assigned in the IDLE case, causing a latch to maintain its previous value [3, 5].
fix_replacement: always_comb begin busy_bit = 0; case(state) ... endcase end
description: Combinational logic pitfall where unassigned states create hardware registers.

title: Asynchronous FIFO Binary Pointer CDC
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [CDC_FAIL] Multi-bit transition sampled as illegal value
expected_root_cause: Passing binary pointers across clock domains allows multiple bits to change, leading to metastability and incorrect sampling [11, 28].
fix_replacement: bin_to_gray(wr_ptr, wr_ptr_gray); sync_gray_ptr(wr_ptr_gray, wr_ptr_gray_sync);
description: Clock Domain Crossing failure due to non-Gray encoded pointer synchronization.

title: Mealy Output Out of Sync with State
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_PROT] DONE pulsed without valid transfer
testbench: initial begin state = IDLE; finish = 1; #10 state = DONE; finish = 0; end
expected_root_cause: The output is high during the transition but the state update logic and output logic are evaluated in different delta cycles [15, 29].
fix_replacement: always_comb begin done = (state == IDLE && next_state == DONE); end
description: Functional error where a status pulse occurs slightly before or after its associated state.

title: Combinational Grant Pulse Glitch
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [PROT_CHECK] GNT asserted for 0.1ns
expected_root_cause: Grant signals are purely combinational and pulse during priority rotation before settling [11, 30].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) gnt <= (req_comb & mask_comb);
description: Timing hazard where combinational logic transitions cause illegal short-duration grant pulses.

title: FSM Power-on Reset Logic Unknown
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [FSM_ERROR] State is X after Power-on
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) state <= next_state; // No reset branch
expected_root_cause: The FSM register lacks a reset signal, keeping the state machine in an 'X' state until the first valid transition [2, 31].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if(!rst_n) state <= IDLE; else ...
description: Initialization bug where the design remains uninitialized without an explicit reset path.

title: FIFO Write Pointer Skip on Full
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Data lost at address 8
expected_root_cause: Write pointer increments even when the FIFO is full, causing data loss and pointer-scoreboard mismatch [6, 32].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (wr_en && !full) wr_ptr <= wr_ptr + 1;
description: Functional bug where internal pointers drift from actual buffer content when backpressure is ignored.

title: FSM Unique Case Runtime Violation
bug_signature: RT-ERROR: More than one case item matches (unique case)
rtl: unique case(1'b1) state: next=S1; state[7]: next=S2; endcase
expected_root_cause: The use of 'unique' asserts that only one bit is set; if the hardware enters a multi-bit state, simulation triggers a runtime error [20, 33].
fix_replacement: priority case (1'b1) state: ... state[7]: ... endcase
description: Verification check failure where overlapping conditions in a case statement are detected at runtime.

title: Incorrect State Value After Reset Pulse
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_ERR] Expected state BOOT (0), Got IDLE (1)
expected_root_cause: The reset branch initializes the FSM to the wrong state according to the design specification [2, 14].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if(!rst_n) state <= BOOT; ...
description: Initialization logic bug where the design starts in an active state rather than the standby state.

title: FIFO Full/Empty False Positive (Race)
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_PROT] Empty and Full both high
expected_root_cause: A parameterized depth of 0 is illegal but not checked, causing both status flags to be true simultaneously [6, 34].
fix_replacement: generate if (DEPTH > 0) ... else initial $error("Illegal Depth"); endgenerate
description: Parameterization bug where edge cases lead to contradictory status signals.

title: FSM Deadlock in WAIT State
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [TIMEOUT] FSM stuck in state WAIT for 10k cycles
rtl: WAIT: if (ack) next = IDLE; else next = WAIT;
testbench: task run(); drive_req(); // ack never arrives endtask
expected_root_cause: The state machine lacks a timeout or abort mechanism to exit the wait state if the handshake partner fails [35, 36].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(state==WAIT) timer <= timer + 1; ... if(timer > LIMIT) next = IDLE;
description: System hang where an FSM remains in a pending state indefinitely.

title: FIFO Data Latency Mismatch on Read
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Got 0xAA, Expected 0xBB (Stale)
testbench: task run(); push(0xAA); push(0xBB); pop(); pop(); endtask
expected_root_cause: The read data bus is registered while the read pointer moves combinatorially, creating a 1-cycle data delay mismatch [6, 23].
fix_replacement: assign rd_data = mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_W-1:0]]; // Use direct array access
description: Timing bug where the data presented on the bus is one cycle behind the read pointer.

title: SVA Stability Check Failure on Reset
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_ERR] Signal changed while reset active
expected_root_cause: The assertion checks for stability without excluding the reset period, leading to false failures [21, 37].
fix_replacement: assert property (@(posedge clk) disable iff (reset) valid |-> $stable(data));
description: Verification environment bug where reset transitions trigger false-positive assertions.

title: Round-Robin Unfair Weighted Grant
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [BW_CHECK] M0 received 90% bandwidth, Expected 50%
testbench: task run(); fork m0.high(); m1.high(); join endtask
expected_root_cause: The priority mask generation is biased toward Master 0, failing to achieve true fairness [38, 39].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(any_gnt) priority_ptr <= (priority_ptr + 1) % NUM_MASTERS;
description: Arbiter fairness bug where the priority rotation is not symmetric.

title: Mealy State-Input Delta Cycle Race
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_MISC] FSM_OUT high but state is IDLE
expected_root_cause: Evaluating 'next_state' in a combinational block for a Mealy output creates a race with the sequential state register [15, 29].
fix_replacement: always_comb begin case(state) IDLE: fsm_out = input; ... endcase end
description: Simulation race condition where internal signals update in an unstable order.

title: FIFO Counter Underflow on Reset
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_ERR] FIFO count is 4'hF (Underflow)
expected_root_cause: Count register decrements on reset or when empty, wrapping to max value [6, 29].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if(!rst_n) count <= 0; else if(rd_en && !empty) count <= count - 1;
description: Functional bug where the occupancy counter becomes corrupted due to unguarded reads.

title: FSM Transient Pulse on Unassigned State
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_GLITCH] ACK pulsed in illegal state
rtl: always_comb case(state) S2: ack = 1; default: ; endcase
testbench: initial begin state = S2; #10 state = 3'b111; end
expected_root_cause: Combinational output logic does not explicitly zero signals in the default case, causing transients [4, 5].
fix_replacement: always_comb begin ack = 0; case(state) S2: ack = 1; endcase end
description: Combinational logic bug where undefined states hold stale high values.

title: Arbiter Grant-Req Hold Violation
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [PROT] REQ dropped while GNT was high
expected_root_cause: The arbiter does not register the request, allowing the master to drop 'req' before the handshake is acknowledged [30, 40].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) req_q <= req; assign gnt = req_q & mask;
description: Protocol handshake failure where signals are dropped before synchronization.

title: FSM Multiple Sequential Blocks Driven
bug_signature: VCS-ERROR: Multi-driver contention on 'state'
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) state <= n1; always_ff @(posedge clk) state <= n2;
expected_root_cause: The same state variable is driven by two separate sequential processes, which is illegal in synthesizable Verilog [2, 29].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(cond) state <= RUN; else state <= IDLE;
description: Compiler error where a register is updated by competing logic blocks.

title: FIFO False Empty Assertion During Pop
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_ERR] Empty high but data in buffer
testbench: task run(); repeat(16) push(); pop(); endtask
expected_root_cause: Empty logic does not check the MSB, causing the flag to assert when the buffer is full (pointers equal) [6].
fix_replacement: assign empty = (wr_ptr == rd_ptr); // wr_ptr and rd_ptr include extra bit
description: Logic error where 'Full' and 'Empty' states are indistinguishable to the hardware.

title: FSM Registered Output Lags State
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_VAL] DONE expected cycle 10, Got cycle 11
testbench: task run(); wait(state == DONE); #1 assert(done == 1); endtask
expected_root_cause: The output is registered based on the current state, creating a 1-cycle lag between the state transition and the signal change [5, 15].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) done <= (next_state == DONE); // Lookahead registration
description: Latency bug where a status signal is delayed relative to the internal state change.

title: Arbiter Mask Incrementer Overflow
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FAIRNESS] Master 0 always wins after cycle 100
expected_root_cause: Priority pointer increments past the number of masters, causing the rotation logic to fail or wrap inconsistently [1, 8].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(any_gnt) last_gnt <= (current_gnt == 2) ? 0 : current_gnt + 1;
description: Fairness bug where the priority rotation pointer enters an illegal range.

title: Casez Priority Reversal Bug
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FSM_STATE] Reset took priority over Start
expected_root_cause: The casez statement has unintentional priority order; 'RUN' matches before 'IDLE' due to item position [17, 20].
fix_replacement: if(ctrl[7]) next=IDLE; else if(ctrl) next=RUN;
description: Functional bug where overlapping wildcards cause the wrong logic branch to execute.

title: FIFO Memory Buffer Index Overflow
bug_signature: VCS-ERROR: Array index 16 is out of bounds [0:15]
rtl: logic [31:0] mem [41]; mem[wr_ptr] <= data; // wr_ptr is 5-bit
expected_root_cause: The write pointer MSB (used for full flag) is used to index a memory that is sized only for the lower bits [6, 31].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(wr_en) mem[wr_ptr[3:0]] <= data;
description: Memory corruption bug where the status bit of a pointer is used as a physical address.

title: Delta Cycle Mismatch in Edge Detection
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FSM_VAL] Edge missed at cycle 50
rtl: assign edge = sig & !sig_reg; always_ff @(posedge clk) sig_reg <= sig;
expected_root_cause: Combinational edge detection is sampled by the FSM in the same cycle as the register update, leading to non-deterministic behavior [29, 42].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin sig_q <= sig; if(sig && !sig_q) next_state = DETECT; end
description: Simulation race condition where edge pulses are too narrow for the state machine to sample.

title: Arbiter Fixed-Priority Master Hog
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [TIMEOUT] Slave 1 never received data
expected_root_cause: The arbiter is implemented as fixed priority; a high-bandwidth master can completely block others indefinitely [8, 20].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(gnt) last_gnt <= ~last_gnt; // Flip priority
description: Starvation bug where Master 0 'hogs' the bus due to non-rotating priority logic.

title: Moore Output Mismatch during State Transition
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_ERR] OUT high in state S0
testbench: initial begin state = S1; #1 state = S0; end
expected_root_cause: The output is purely combinational based on state; during reset or forcing, the output can pulse incorrectly [5, 15].
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(!rst_n) fsm_out <= 0; else fsm_out <= (next_state == S1);
description: Timing hazard where state transitions create glitchy combinational outputs.

title: FIFO False Empty Assertion (MSB mismatch)
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [SVA_ERR] Read from Empty FIFO at T=100
expected_root_cause: The empty logic is accidentally using the 'Full' logic formula, asserting only when the buffer is full [6, 32].
fix_replacement: assign empty = (wr_ptr == rd_ptr);
description: Safety violation where the FIFO allows reads only when full and blocks when empty.
